description: JMeter is my veteran load testing companion — I design thread groups, parameterize test data, configure listeners, and simulate thousands of concurrent users hammering APIs under realistic conditions.
I pair Grafana with performance test pipelines to visualize live metrics — response time percentiles, error rates, and throughput trends rendered in real-time dashboards during load runs.
K6 is my modern performance scripting tool — writing load scenarios in JavaScript feels natural, and its CI-friendly output integrates seamlessly into GitHub Actions pipelines.
Artillery lets me define load scenarios in clean YAML configs — I use it for rapid HTTP and WebSocket performance testing with minimal setup and cloud-scale execution.
BlazeMeter gives me cloud-scale load testing without managing infrastructure — I run JMeter and Gatling scripts at massive concurrency levels and share detailed reports with stakeholders instantly.
NeoLoad brings enterprise-grade performance testing to complex application stacks — I use it for protocol-level load simulation and SLA-based pass/fail validation in regulated environments.
JSON is the data backbone of every performance test I run — I craft dynamic payloads, parse response bodies, and feed parameterized datasets into load scenarios with precision.
I use HTML as the canvas for custom performance reports — structuring test summaries, metric tables, and threshold breach highlights into clean, shareable documents for every stakeholder.
CSS brings my performance reports to life — styled metric cards, color-coded pass/fail indicators, and responsive layouts that make dense test data instantly readable and professional.
JavaScript powers the interactive layer of my custom reports and K6 test scripts alike — dynamic charts, collapsible sections, and live metric filtering all driven by vanilla JS.
When no off-the-shelf report tells the full story, I build one from scratch — branded HTML dashboards with interactive charts, SLA comparisons, and drill-down breakdowns tailored for each client.
